# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Node Package Installation & Build with cache ⏳️🌟​√​​˙‍!~~^~﻿# ~(¬_↵)™€@®¶©+•–°…÷Ø‘'''"" """<<<>>> ☃ℝ𝐎×
uses: actions/cache/.setup-node.js  # You can use any other GitHub Actions theme for setting up node version with specific versions (using 'actions/checkout@v2') and caching packages dependencies to speedup CI pipeline below step is commented out because we're not using a v1 container here
with:   # Set these values as per requirement. They are set in the cache-dependency settings block under workflow name section of YAML file, if you want it globally replace 'actions/checkout@v2', and checkboxes below should be unchecked for caching all packages or just Node_modules
  nodeVersion: "14" # You can use any version number here. It will affect every run since we're not doing anything in the setup step that requires specific dependencies versions to have a consistent behavior across runs e github actions cache is an instance of AWS S3 and uses key-value pairs which are unique for each file
  checkAllPackages: "false" # Unchecked if all packages should be cached or just Node_modules, otherwise it's checked. Recommended to use 'true'. You can also specify paths as a comma delimited list like so -> cache-dependency:   keys=<pattern>
  fetchDepth: auto    # Fetch the latest commit instead of just the history so you get newer changes in case there are any conflicts between your actions. Can be set to 'auto', or specific number e g '-1' which means no fetching and install packages from remote repository can make sense if not under version control
env:   # You may add environment-specific configs here but it is optional based on requirement, usually they are required depending upon application nature. Can be used in combination with setup step for defining different environments like staging/production etc (based off pattern) to define cache keys and patterns that can influence the action of a workflow run
